items.title: Make Launch Readiness truth-state driven instead of permanently blocked
items.insight: The Settings cockpit correctly exposes the Cloudflare/GitHub Pages outage, but its public-domain row is hardcoded to Blocked. That protects the current beta gate today and becomes a lying surface the moment the domain is fixed, so the row needs a small status model that can represent blocked, ready, and needs-check evidence.
items.recipe: Add a pure public-domain readiness resolver to `buildLaunchReadinessRows()`, accept explicit status evidence, preserve the known blocker as the default, and add unit coverage for blocked, ready, and stale/needs-check states.

items.title: Turn Draft War Room targets into steal-risk decisions
items.insight: The Draft War Room now ranks useful targets, but it still stops short of the emotional decision a General Manager needs: can I wait, or is this player about to disappear? Adding steal-risk labels from board rank, user pick distance, projected round, and roster need makes the panel feel like a live room instead of a smarter table.
items.recipe: Extend `buildDraftPressureModel()` targets with `stealRisk`, `urgency`, and a pressure chip; render those labels on target cards; test critical user-on-clock and low-risk waiting states.

items.title: Attach launch-readiness context to beta feedback
items.insight: Tester reports are more valuable when they arrive with the same launch posture the game is showing locally. A compact readiness packet in the GitHub issue body lets the founder distinguish gameplay reports from deployment-access reports without a backend, account system, or analytics dependency.
items.recipe: Teach `buildFeedbackIssueUrl()` to include optional readiness rows, gather a static readiness snapshot when opening feedback, and add tests that the generated issue body carries runtime/domain context without personal data.

skipped.reason: Still blocked locally by missing Cloudflare/GitHub repo capabilities; keep the runbook and blocker-preflight evidence rather than pretending the game repo can mutate the shared org-root domain.
The idea remains strong, but it should be trained from post-beta decision telemetry so it critiques real player habits instead of invented ones.

executionLog.evidence: Added resolvePublicDomainReadiness(), wired Launch Readiness to explicit public-domain evidence, and covered blocked/ready/needs-check states.
Draft War Room targets now include stealRisk and urgency, render the steal-risk label, and pass critical plus low-risk wait tests.
Feedback issue URLs can embed launch-readiness rows; runtime shard passed 75/75.
